title: LifeOS direct database control surface defined
objective: Define the LifeOS Mission Control path that reads, directs, and advances the canonical PostgreSQL state through local UDS and redb-backed low-latency views, so every visible state and command maps to a canonical database identity, explicit authority, a UDS request/response contract, labeled freshness, a witness-chain audit event, a rollback, and an enumerated non-hidden filesystem path — with no HTTP and no flat-file control-plane detour.

work_items.surface: Read path (NS-CLARITY): 10 visible Mission Control states
work_items.decision: Enumerate RS-01..RS-10 (swarm live status, database-hosted codebase, semantic/hybrid search, COW branch tree, ATAS future timelines, MinCut integrity, witness-chain audit trail, RuvLTRA routing telemetry, ingestion status, materialization status); each renders from a canonical PostgreSQL identity or an explicitly-labeled redb-live/transient-derived view with read-provenance lineage.

Enumerate CS-01..CS-08 (semantic edit, commit/merge, discard, dispatch/route, sever/isolate, ingest/index, materialize/release, simulate/forecast); each names exactly one authority, targets a canonical database identity, and carries a SHAKE256 witness-chain audit event and a defined rollback.

work_items.surface: UDS transport contract (no HTTP, no flat file)
work_items.decision: Define two channels: a MessagePack-framed control UDS (SOCK_STREAM, request {method,params,request_id} / response {result|error,audit_ref}) served by the envctl runtime bridge, and a read-only zero-copy redb mmap for microsecond live views. Forbid any HTTP/REST listener and any flat-file control-plane spool.

Adopt three labeled freshness tiers (redb-live microseconds, postgres-canonical milliseconds, transient-derived) and require every surface to name exactly one authoritative owner, keeping LifeOS a reader + command issuer only per the STORE-001 single-durable-writer and forward-only-sync invariants.

Enumerate all three reachable filesystem writes and classify each non-hidden: the derived/disposable envctl CS-07 projection, the agent-written read-only redb runtime file, and the transitional non-canonical Tauri UI view-state whitelist (verified in src/lib/persistence.ts). Establish CS-07 projection as the sole sanctioned LifeOS-triggered filesystem write.

Run a live gate-check over the plan JSON asserting all seven gate fields on every state and command, no_http, no_flat_file, filesystem writes enumerated non-hidden, and a witness-chain audit on every command; record the exit-0 result and STORE-001 alignment in proof_records/POSTGRES-007.proof.json.

next_action: Decompose read, command, status, audit, and recovery paths into bounded vertical slices after graph closure; refine canonical_identity field/table names once POSTGRES-003 and POSTGRES-006 plans materialize.
completion_gate: generated/lifeos_uds_database_surface_plan.json enumerates every visible state and command with canonical database identity, explicit authority, UDS request/response contract, freshness semantics, audit event, rollback, and an enumerated non-hidden filesystem write path; POSTGRES-007.proof.json records the passing gate simulation with product_execution_performed false.
